rulings.Case Title: DOLLY TOTES VS. CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A PUBLIC ENTITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Disocvery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 22, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF DOLLY TOTES' Motion To Compel Responses To Disocvery is Granted in part. Defendant shall serve by personal delivery, email or fax further responses to RFAs 15 - 18 admitting, denying and/or stating the reasons in detail why it is unable to answer no later than 5pm March 23, 2012. Defendant's responses to RFAs 46 and 47 are sufficient. Monetary sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: CAMERON RODRIGUEZ VS. WINCOR NIXDORF, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 22, 2012, Line 3, DEFENDANT WINCOR NIXDORF, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT - The motion for summary judgment is denied and the alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the negligence and assault and battery claims and denied as to the negligent hiring and supervision claims. As to the negligence and assault and battery causes of action, Wincor satisfied it burden of production that it is not liable under respondeat superior principles and plaintiff did not present any evidence creating a triable issue to the contrary. As to the negligent hiring and supervison claim, there is a triable issue whether Wincor should have known of Mr. Kane's violent propensities. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: ECONOMY FOODS, INC., A CORPORATION DBA FACCIOLA VS. WEINETERS LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY DBA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 22, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF ECONOMY FOODS, INC., A CORPORATION, DBA FACCIOLA MEAT COMPANY'S Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TODD GELFAND, TRUSTEE OF THE REATA TRUST VS. NORTH AMERICAN CAPACITY INSURANCE CO.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Atty'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 22, 2012, Line 8, RESPONDENT NORTH AMERICAN CAPACITY INSURANCE CO. Ntc Of Mtn For Atty'S Fees is Denied. Civil Code 1717 does not apply as to Respondent North American Capacity and does not entitle it to attorney fees. Although section 1717 has been employed to award attorney fees to prevailing parties even in situations where the contract containing the attorney fees provisions is unenforceable, rescinded, or nonexistent, or where the party sued on a contract is actually a nonsignatory, North American Capacity was not a party to the contract and was not a party to the underlying arbitration action to enforce the contract. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: TODD GELFAND, TRUSTEE OF THE REATA TRUST VS. NORTH AMERICAN CAPACITY INSURANCE CO.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Attys' Fee; Memo Of P&A In Support Thereof; Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 22, 2012, Line 8, PETITIONER TODD GELFAND, TRUSTEE OF THE REATA TRUST Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Attys' Fee is Granted in part and denied in part. Petitioner is awarded $25,000. Petitioner is entitled to fees under Code of Civil Procedure 1293.2, and 1033.5(a)(10) and Civil Code 1717(a)). However, the amount sought is unreasonable. $25,000 is a reasonable fee award. rulings.Case Title: TODD GELFAND, TRUSTEE OF THE REATA TRUST VS. NORTH AMERICAN CAPACITY INSURANCE CO.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Tax Costs; Req. For Judicial Ntc And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 22, 2012, Line 8, RESPONDENT NORTH AMERICAN CAPACITY INSURANCE CO. Ntc Of Mtn To Tax Costs is Denied. Petitioner only seeks costs as to Respondent Paragon. Petitioner was required to name all of the parties to the arbitration as respondents to its petition to confirm arbitration award. (Code of Civil Procedure 1285). Therefore, Petitioner is entitled to recover the costs of that service. (Code of Civil Procedure 1033.5(a)(4)). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: BETTY ANN MORRIS VS. RESIDENTIAL RENT STABLIZATION AND ARBITRATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Writ Of Mandate; Memo Of P & A; Dec And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 22, 2012, Line 10, PETITIONER BETTY MORRIS' motion For Writ Of Mandate is Granted. After being apprised of the pending lawsuit in this court filed by Ms. Morris and others against Ms. Greaux seeking to declare the lease void and to establish rent for the premises, the Rent Board should not have taken any further action on Ms. Greaux's rent reduction petition. The pending lawsuit and the rent reduction petition are inextricably intertwined and thus concern the same subject matter. This court has the authority to grant all the relief sought by Ms. Greaux in her petition to the Rent Board. Moreover, by bringing this petition, Ms. Morris is estopped from arguing otherwise. By rejecting Ms. Morris' objections based on "jurisdiction," the Rent Board prejudicially abused its discretion within the meaning of Code of Civil Procedure 1094.5(b). The parties are encouraged to enter into a stipulation permitting Ms. Greaux, if she desires, to file amended pleadings in the pending lawsuit alleging the claims she made in her petition to the Rent Board. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: NASIR JAVID et al VS. CHUNCHU CHANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Otion To Change Venue, And Memo Of P&A; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 22, 2012, Line 12, DEFENDANTs CHANG, CHUN CHU "LINDA", THAI, HIEU "KELVIN" Motion To Change Venue is Denied. The court has been hampered in ruling on this motion by the failure of the parties to provide what may be essential information. The court has not received copies of the lease except for the page containing paragraph 29 or the declaration of Kelvin Thai. Based on the materials the court has received (the moving papers filed February 28, 2012, the opposition papers to the prior motion filed February 8, 2012 and the complaint without any exhibits), the defendants have not satisfied their burden of showing that venue is improper in San Francisco. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/HEK)
